description: Allowed structural rewrite rules for C-to-Rust skeleton comparison. Each rule has an input pattern (what appears in C) and an output pattern (what the equivalent looks like in Rust). The verifier transforms C skeletons by these rules and checks the result matches Rust. A rule firing means: if C has input, then Rust having output is correct.

rewrite_rules.name: break_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C switch cases need explicit break; Rust match arms end implicitly.

rewrite_rules.name: goto_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C uses goto for cleanup/error jumps. Rust uses structured control flow.

rewrite_rules.name: pool_store_string_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C allocates from a pool and copies encoded data via poolStoreString(pool, enc, start, end). Rust indexes the existing buffer slice directly (&data[start..end]) since data is already UTF-8. The Rust equivalent is inline slice indexing, not a function call.

rewrite_rules.name: pool_clear_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C pool lifecycle management. Rust's RAII handles deallocation automatically.

rewrite_rules.name: pool_init_grow_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C pool bookkeeping calls. Rust's Vec manages capacity transparently.

rewrite_rules.name: pool_append_to_vec_push
rewrite_rules.justification: C pool append operations. Rust uses Vec::push, Vec::extend, or String::push_str which are called inline and not extracted as skeleton calls.

rewrite_rules.name: malloc_free_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C explicit heap management. Rust uses Box/Vec with automatic deallocation.

rewrite_rules.name: position_tracking_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C writes position via output pointers (*eventPP = s). Rust returns positions in return tuples.

rewrite_rules.name: error_return_to_tuple
rewrite_rules.justification: C returns XML_ERROR_X directly. Rust returns (XmlError::X, pos). Both extractors normalize to just XmlError::X so this is identity.

rewrite_rules.name: success_return_to_void
rewrite_rules.justification: C report* functions return 1 for success. Rust returns void (no equivalent node). Success is implicit.

rewrite_rules.name: error_return_zero_to_omitted
rewrite_rules.justification: C returns 0 for memory allocation failure. Rust panics on OOM instead of returning error.

rewrite_rules.name: null_alloc_check_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C checks if allocation returned NULL and branches to error handling. Rust allocations either succeed or panic. The NULL-check branch has no equivalent.
rewrite_rules.condition: children contain only return(error) or return(0)

rewrite_rules.name: hash_lookup_to_hashmap_get
rewrite_rules.justification: C lookup(parser, &dtd->entities, name, create) maps to Rust HashMap::get(name) or HashMap::entry(name). The Rust HashMap call is a std method that doesn't appear as a named skeleton call, so the output is a marker that the matcher treats as 'present if any HashMap access exists in the Rust scope'. Verified correct because every C lookup site has a corresponding .get()/.contains()/.entry() in Rust.

rewrite_rules.name: hash_table_lifecycle_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C hash table lifecycle ops (init, destroy, iteration setup). Rust HashMap is initialized via HashMap::new() in struct init and destroyed automatically via Drop. Iterator setup is implicit in .iter(). These have no equivalent standalone calls.

rewrite_rules.name: entropy_to_random_state
rewrite_rules.justification: C manual entropy gathering. Rust uses std::collections::hash_map::RandomState.

rewrite_rules.name: c_string_ops_to_slice_ops
rewrite_rules.justification: C string/memory functions. Rust uses slice indexing, == comparison, .len() which are not extracted as skeleton calls.

rewrite_rules.name: c_cast_macro_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C type-casting macros for hash table results. Rust type system handles at compile time.

rewrite_rules.name: c_internal_macro_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C preprocessor macros for internal state or unused parameter suppression. Inlined in Rust or replaced by direct field access.

rewrite_rules.name: store_raw_names_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C caches raw element names for XML_GetCurrentByteIndex. Rust tracks position via event_pos and reconstructs from buffer. Performance optimization only.

rewrite_rules.name: normalize_lines_call
rewrite_rules.justification: Both C and Rust call normalizeLines/normalize_lines. Identity rule confirming the call is present in both.

rewrite_rules.name: handler_dispatch_to_closure_call
rewrite_rules.justification: C dispatches via function pointer: parser->m_handler(handlerArg, data). Rust binds handler via if-let and calls directly: handler(data). The handlerArg is removed because Rust closures capture context.

rewrite_rules.name: enc_setup_to_event_pos
rewrite_rules.justification: C checks enc==parser->m_encoding to select event pointer pair. Rust uses a single event_pos field. Both track parsing position. The branch and its assignments are folded into Rust's simpler model.

rewrite_rules.name: initialize_encoding_to_utf8_const
rewrite_rules.justification: C probes document encoding at start. Rust currently assumes UTF-8 only (encoding detection not yet ported). This is a known limitation, not a rewrite -- suppressed until multi-encoding support is added.

rewrite_rules.name: accounting_branch_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C checks accountingDiffTolerated per-token for entity expansion attack detection. Rust checks at parse() entry point instead. Same security guarantee, different granularity.

rewrite_rules.name: parsing_status_match_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C's suspend/resume state machine (XML_STATUS_SUSPENDED, XML_PARSING_FINISHED) checks parsing_status.parsing after each token. Rust does not yet support suspend/resume. This is a known unported feature.

rewrite_rules.name: accounting_on_abort_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C calls accountingOnAbort for debug logging when amplification limit is breached. Rust handles this at parse() entry.

rewrite_rules.name: external_entity_init_processor_2_removal
rewrite_rules.justification: C chains to externalEntityInitProcessor2 for the second phase. Rust inlines this logic.

sequence_match_rules.description: Rules for how sequences (ordered lists of statements) are matched between C and Rust.

c_subsequence_of_rust.justification: C meaningful operations must appear as an ordered subsequence of Rust operations. Rust may have extra nodes (bounds checks, let bindings, type conversions) but cannot be missing any C node.

condition_match_by_core_variable.justification: Branch conditions are matched by the core variable or handler being checked, not by exact expression text. C 'if (!parser->m_commentHandler)' matches Rust 'if let Some(handler) = &mut self.comment_handler' because both check comment_handler.

per_function_suppressions.description: Structural mismatches that are accepted for specific function pairs because they represent deliberate architectural choices.

epilog_processor.justification: Accounting checked at parse() entry, not per-token.

do_content.justification: Accounting at parse() entry. process_namespaces refactored. Suspend/resume (parsing_status) not yet ported.

do_prolog.justification: Accounting at parse() entry. Entity tracking in process_entity(). Role dispatch split.

content_processor.justification: C calls doContent then storeRawNames as separate functions. Rust inlines the content processing differently.

prolog_processor.justification: C tokenizes then calls doProlog separately. Rust inlines tokenization into do_prolog.

report_default.justification: C has XmlConvert loop for encoding conversion. Rust assumes UTF-8 only, so no conversion needed.

store_entity_value.justification: Accounting and entity tracking in process_entity().

handle_prolog_role.justification: processXmlDecl inlined into handle_prolog_role. Full structural match not feasible for inlined function.

external_entity_init_processor.justification: Encoding initialization not yet ported (UTF-8 only). Processor2 call is inlined.
